fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
Sachin Tendulkar of India is the highest run-scorer in international cricket history, scoring 100 international centuries (51 in Tests, 49 in ODIs) before retiring in 2013.
The West Indies won the first two ICC Cricket World Cups in 1975 and 1979 under the captaincy of Clive Lloyd. Both finals were played at Lord's in London.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0 in the final.
Badminton made its official Olympic debut as a medal sport at the 1992 Summer Games in Barcelona, Spain. Indonesia's Susi Susanti won the first-ever women's singles gold medal.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Pelé of Brazil is the only player in football history to win three FIFA World Cup titles as a player, claiming victories in 1958, 1962, and 1970.
In the 2022 FIFA World Cup held in Qatar, Argentina won their third World Cup title by defeating France 4-2 on penalties after a dramatic 3-3 draw in extra time.
The National Basketball Association (NBA) was founded in New York City on June 6, 1946, originally named the Basketball Association of America (BAA) before merging with the NBL.
Michael Jordan led the Chicago Bulls to six NBA championships (1991–1993 and 1996–1998) and won the Finals MVP award in all six championships, a league record.
The Wimbledon Championship, founded in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world. It is the only Grand Slam tournament played on outdoor grass courts.
Roger Federer was the first male tennis player to win 20 Grand Slam singles titles. He achieved this milestone at the 2018 Australian Open.
Rafael Nadal of Spain holds the record for the most titles won at a single Grand Slam tournament, with 14 French Open (Roland Garros) men's singles championships.
